name: cmospwd
title: cmospwd
description:
decrypt BIOS passwords from CMOS
CmosPwd is a cross-platform tool to decrypt password stored in CMOS used
to access a computer's BIOS setup.
.
This application should work out of the box on most modern systems, but
some more esoteric BIOSes may not be supported or may require additional
steps.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cmospwd because decrypt BIOS passwords from CMOS.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cmospwd as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cmospwd [options]...` as documented upstream for cmospwd.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cmospwd with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cmospwd --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: cmospwd - a cmos/bios password recovery tool
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install cmospwd. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cmospwd [options]...`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: decrypt BIOS passwords from CMOS.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: cmospwd - a cmos/bios password recovery tool.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install cmospwd
Install with: sudo apt install cmospwd
